checks.langcore_canonical_equal: resolved root AST modulo statementId equal between src and decode(encode(src)) via the official bridge
checks.dsl_tokenizer_canonicalize_idempotence: canonicalize(canonicalize(src)) == canonicalize(src) with fresh per-example symbol tables

divergences_fixed: dead-binding root mislabel: encoder ordered unreferenced statements after root while the decoder names the last stream statement 'root'; decoded programs bound root to the wrong expression

name-sensitive statement order: _statement_order scanned refs with a regex over surface text, so binder names occurring inside string literals changed the stream; the decoder's alpha-renaming then re-encoded to a different token stream than the training target

children-first prop emission: _encode_component_props emitted children before the declared positional prop order, so decoded surfaces reparsed with values assigned to the wrong props (Modal children landed in title)
v0.5 decode reorder: _decode_v05 moved root to the first output line while the v0.5 encoder preserves source statement order, breaking token idempotence for state-first programs
fail_closed: forward references (including references to root, which is now always the final statement) raise ParseError at encode instead of emitting a stream that decodes to a different program
caveats: fixture-corpus wiring evidence only; no checkpoint, matrix, or ship claim
production token streams changed for programs hit by the fixed divergences, so X-series checkpoints trained on pre-fix streams need retraining before comparison
eval-side exact-match asymmetry remains: eval_runner._tree_match compares codec-canonical predictions against echo-normalized gold; deferred to D2 (e-graph canonicalizer, SLM-30)
conclusion: encode_openui token streams are now fixed points of the deterministic decode collapse, verified per-record over both fixture corpora and canonically equal (resolved AST modulo statementId) through the official lang-core bridge; B1 choice-sequence work can build on a codec whose training targets and decode-time collapse agree.
